name: Lotus Barge
region: Asia, Sri Lanka, Western Province, Colombo
ocean: Asia, Sri Lanka, Western Province, Colombo
description: A sunken barge resting at 18 meters, this artificial reef teems with marine life. Schools of snapper and batfish swirl around the wreck, while moray eels peek from crevices. The structure is encrusted with soft corals and sponges, attracting colorful reef fish. Visibility varies with currents, offering a dynamic dive. Ideal for intermediate divers, the site provides shelter for critters like nudibranchs and shrimp. The shallow depth allows extended bottom time to explore the thriving ecosystem. Currents can be strong, bringing pelagics like trevally. Close to Colombo, it's a convenient escape into underwater adventure.

name: Pigeon Rock
ocean: Indian Ocean, Bay Of Bengal
description: This dive site off Sri Lanka's coast features dramatic granite boulders and swim-throughs teeming with marine life. Expect strong currents that attract pelagics like barracuda, trevally, and reef sharks. The rocky outcrops host vibrant soft corals and macro critters. Visibility varies but often exceeds 20 meters. Best suited for experienced divers due to challenging conditions.

name: Diyamba Kupatha
ocean: Indian Ocean, Laccadive Sea, Gulf Of Mannar
description: This site off Sri Lanka's coast offers vibrant coral gardens teeming with reef fish, moray eels, and occasional turtles. The sloping reef transitions to sandy patches where stingrays and octopuses hide. Visibility ranges 10-20 meters, with mild currents ideal for beginners. The warm Indian Ocean waters host colorful soft corals and barrel sponges. Night dives reveal crustaceans and hunting lionfish. Depths reach 18 meters, suitable for all certification levels. The Gulf of Mannar's sheltered location ensures calm conditions most of the year.

name: Goda Gala
ocean: Indian Ocean, Laccadive Sea, Gulf Of Mannar
description: This site features a stunning coral garden with vibrant hard and soft corals. Schools of tropical fish dart between the formations, while moray eels peek from crevices. The gentle current makes for an easy drift dive. Visibility often exceeds 20 meters, revealing the rich marine biodiversity. Turtles and reef sharks are frequent visitors. The sloping reef transitions to sandy patches where garden eels sway. Suitable for all skill levels, with depths ranging from 5 to 30 meters. The warm waters remain comfortable year-round.
